research.product: NetApp ONTAP REST API (ONTAP 9.18.1 reference; consistency groups GA since 9.10.1)
research.interface: ONTAP REST API /api/application/consistency-groups with /api/cluster/jobs asynchronous job pattern

official_sources.title: Application consistency-groups endpoint overview (NetApp official docs source for docs.netapp.com/us-en/ontap-restapi/ontap/application_consistency-groups_endpoint_overview.html)
official_sources.used_for: collection GET with svm.name filtering and fields=*/**, instance GET /api/application/consistency-groups/{uuid}, POST/PATCH being asynchronous job operations, volumes with provisioning_options for adding existing volumes, and DELETE removing only the group while storage persists

official_sources.title: Create a consistency group (POST /api/application/consistency-groups)
official_sources.used_for: required svm.uuid|svm.name plus volumes, optional name/qos.policy/snapshot_policy, return_timeout default 0 giving 202 with a job object, and error 53411860 'An object with the same identifier in the same scope exists'

official_sources.title: Update a consistency group (PATCH /api/application/consistency-groups/{uuid})
official_sources.used_for: PATCH by uuid supporting adding volumes via provisioning_options and qos.policy.name changes, 202-plus-job responses, and the documented error codes 262285 (no element removal via PATCH), 53411842 ('Consistency group does not exist.'), 53411852/53411853 (identifier and field conflicts)

official_sources.title: Getting started with the ONTAP REST API (NetApp official docs source)
official_sources.used_for: base path /api, HTTP basic authentication, error object {error:{code,message,target}}, collection shape {records,num_records}, and the asynchronous pattern: 202 Accepted with job {uuid,_links.self.href} polled at /api/cluster/jobs/{uuid} until success or failure

official_sources.title: Retrieve details of an asynchronous job (GET /api/cluster/jobs/{uuid})
official_sources.used_for: job record fields uuid/state/message plus error {code,message} populated only on failure; success and failure are terminal

research.language_rationale: Our storage controllers are reconciled from Go operators, and ONTAP's documented Go surface is the REST API itself (NetApp maintains trident and devops-toolkit integrations in Go against this same contract). net/http with httptest exercises the documented surface hermetically with zero dependencies.

catalog_correction: The catalog item asks for ETag/UUID conflict handling. Current official ONTAP REST documentation defines no ETag/If-Match conditional-request semantics for consistency groups; concurrency conflicts surface as documented error codes instead (POST job failure 53411860 'same identifier exists', instance 404 code 53411842 'Consistency group does not exist.', PATCH conflicts 53411852/53411853). The seed pins those documented mechanisms and this correction is noted per wave law.
verified_facts: Consistency groups live at /api/application/consistency-groups; the collection GET supports name and svm.name filters and the instance GET /api/application/consistency-groups/{uuid} supports fields=** for a complete document.
POST requires an SVM reference (svm.name or svm.uuid) and volumes; existing volumes are attached with provisioning_options {action: 'add'}; qos.policy.name assigns a QoS policy at the group level.

POST and PATCH are asynchronous: with return_timeout=0 the server answers 202 Accepted with {job:{uuid,_links.self.href}}; the job is polled on that href until the terminal state success or failure, and a failed job carries error {code, message}.
PATCH by uuid can add volumes and change qos.policy but cannot remove elements (error 262285); extra volumes already in the group must be left alone.

Documented conflict errors replace ETag semantics: 53411860 'An object with the same identifier in the same scope exists' (create race), 53411842 'Consistency group does not exist.' (uuid vanished), 53411852/53411853 (identifier/field conflicts on PATCH).
Errors use the {error:{code,message,target}} object; all requests are HTTP-basic-authenticated under /api.
